figures.caption: Table 2: Model comparison across different loss functions. Values in parentheses indicate the boundary-based counterpart of each metric [30]. (↑) indicates that higher values represent better performance. Relative percentage difference (∆%) is calculated between DDA and the second-best loss. Model input resolutions follow the prior papers.
Fig. 3: Distributions of predicted mask pixel values of the vehicle image in Fig. 2-bottom. Vertical lines point the optimal thresholds θ maximizing given scores (IoU , F1 or Fβ).

Table 1: Quantitative evaluation on several models optimized using DDA loss. Values in parentheses indicate the boundarybased counterpart of each metric [30]. (↑) indicates that higher values represent better performance. ∗Zero-shot models were not fine-tuned on this dataset; tight bounding boxes derived from ground-truth masks were used as prompts.
Fig. 2: Qualitative comparison between models from Tables 1 (top) and 2 (bottom). Best in color and with zooming.
Fig. 1: Linear Discriminant Analysis application on linearly separable (top) and inseparable (bottom) datasets.
